articles.title: The Plan: 212 West 72nd Street Goes Modern on Historic Upper West Side
articles.narrative: Centurion Property Investors is nearing completion of sales at 212 West 72nd Street, a luxury condominium conversion on Manhattan's Upper West Side. The project, which transformed a former rental building, features 106 residences designed by CetraRuddy, with 14 units remaining. The building introduces a modern, curved glass aesthetic to the historic neighborhood, offering unique layouts, high-end finishes, and a suite of amenities.

data_points.address: 212 West 72nd Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Modern curved glass tower; residences feature unique layouts, oversized windows, white oak floors, Miele appliances, wine fridges, Waterworks and Kohler fixtures, and Venus White marble floors. Amenities include a landscaped rooftop terrace with outdoor kitchen, fitness center with outdoor meditation space, residents lounge, and children's play area.
data_points.project_notes: Conversion from a rental building. Prices for available condos range from $4.5 million for a three-bedroom to $13.5 million for a penthouse.

articles.title: 25 Kent Offers Companies A Cutting-Edge Office Experience Along The Williamsburg Waterfront
articles.narrative: 25 Kent, a 500,000-square-foot Class A office building on the Williamsburg waterfront in Brooklyn, is attracting a strong mix of creative and technology companies, particularly those in AI. The building offers flexible floor plans, high-end prebuilt spaces, and custom buildouts, allowing tenants to scale their operations. With amenities like a fitness center, rooftop terrace, and independent HVAC systems, 25 Kent provides a cutting-edge office experience in a desirable location with limited comparable Class A space. The building is currently 72% leased, with asking rents at $76 per square foot, demonstrating its appeal to companies seeking a dynamic live-work environment outside of Manhattan.

data_points.address: 25 Kent Avenue, Brooklyn, NY
data_points.notable_features: New construction Class A office building with 15-foot ceilings, floor-to-ceiling windows, 360-degree waterfront and skyline views, terraces on every level, independent HVAC systems, and a 12,000-square-foot landscaped rooftop terrace.
data_points.project_notes: The building has over 300,000 square feet leased, with a 57,350-square-foot lease signed in January for a 10-year term.

people.name: Ryan Gessin
people.title: head of the 25 Kent leasing team

articles.title: Benchmark Buys Upper West Side Apartment Building From Heller for $42M
articles.narrative: Benchmark Real Estate Group has acquired a 90-unit prewar apartment building on Manhattan's Upper West Side from The Heller Organization for $42 million. This transaction marks Benchmark's second deal with Heller in less than a year, following a $66 million acquisition in September 2025. The property, built in 1925, is located at 698 West End Avenue and features one-bedroom apartments starting at $3,200 per month.

data_points.address: 698 West End Avenue, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Prewar apartment building located at the corner of West End Avenue and West 94th Street.
data_points.project_notes: The Heller Organization previously acquired the property in 1998.

articles.title: AI Marketing Platform Hightouch Takes 18K SF at 275 Seventh Avenue
articles.narrative: Hightouch, a data and AI marketing platform, has relocated its New York City operations to a full-floor, 18,000-square-foot space at 275 Seventh Avenue in Midtown South. The new office will serve as the company's operational base for its sales, engineering, finance, and executive teams, allowing for expanded engagement with its significant customer base in New York. The move represents an expansion for Hightouch, which previously occupied smaller offices in the area.

data_points.address: 275 Seventh Avenue, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Art Deco tower located between West 25th and West 26th streets.
data_points.project_notes: Hightouch previously leased smaller offices at 150 West 22nd Street and 110 West 27th Street. The space was formerly occupied by Tennr.

articles.market_intelligence: Office asking rents in Midtown South averaged $79.86 per square foot in April.

articles.title: Arixa Capital Lends $28M on Hawaii Build-for-Sale Luxury Homes
articles.narrative: West Point Investment Corporation has secured $27.6 million in construction financing from Arixa Capital for its Hapuna Estates project, a luxury single-family home development within the Mauna Kea Resort on Hawaii's Big Island. The project will feature seven for-sale homes on lots ranging from 0.39 to 0.75 acres, with construction expected to begin in early 2027 and delivery slated for 2028. Berkadia arranged the competitive financing, noting strong sales velocity and high demand for the limited remaining custom home lots in this established resort community.

data_points.notable_features: Located within the historic Mauna Kea Resort, developed by Laurance Rockefeller in 1964. Homes will range from 0.39 acres to 0.75 acres.
data_points.project_notes: Construction is slated to begin in early 2027.

articles.title: New Jersey Retail: The Juice Is Worth The Squeeze
articles.narrative: This article argues that New Jersey presents a unique and attractive retail market for operators despite its challenges, which include tight space availability, high rents, and complex regulatory processes. The author highlights that retailers often need to adapt their store formats to fit the market's constraints, particularly in Northern New Jersey where large boxes are rare. Brokers emphasize the importance of local expertise to navigate the state's diverse submarkets and municipal nuances. Despite initial sticker shock on rents, tenants often find strong store performance and favorable occupancy costs, making the investment worthwhile. The article also points out the distinct advantage of New Jersey's retail landscape, which often includes ample parking and a suburban feel even in urban environments, fostering cross-shopping and longer customer stays.

articles.title: Newmark Arranges Sale, Financing of 1.4 MSF Office Campus in Irving
articles.narrative: Newmark has facilitated the sale and acquisition financing for The Towers at Williams Square, a 1.4 million-square-foot office campus located in Irving's Las Colinas district. The property, comprising four buildings, was 76 percent leased at the time of closing. A joint venture between Vanderbilt Office Properties, Hillwood, and TriPost Capital Partners acquired the asset, which recently underwent $25 million in renovations to its common areas and amenity spaces.

data_points.notable_features: Four buildings, three of which are interconnected; recently underwent $25 million in renovations to lobbies, amenity spaces, and common areas.

articles.title: Disney Investment Group Negotiates $82.2M Sale of Retail Power Center in Norman, Oklahoma
articles.narrative: Disney Investment Group has negotiated the $82.2 million sale of University Town Center, a 416,766-square-foot retail power center in Norman, Oklahoma. The property, built in 2009 and 99% leased at the time of sale, was acquired by a partnership between Mazaheri Properties and Champion Hotels from an affiliate of Rainier Cos. Disney Investment Group also brokered the previous sale of the property in 2019.

data_points.address: 1400 24th Ave NW, Norman, OK
data_points.notable_features: Located in Norman, home of the University of Oklahoma.
data_points.project_notes: Disney Investment Group also brokered the sale of the property in 2019.

articles.title: Weitzman LP Acquires 87,857 SF Shopping Center in Arlington
articles.narrative: A limited partnership managed by Weitzman has acquired Cooper Street Annex, an 87,857-square-foot shopping center in Arlington, Texas. The property was fully leased at the time of sale, featuring a strong tenant mix. Weitzman's internal team represented the buyer, while SummitRE represented the undisclosed seller in the transaction.

articles.title: Malabar Hill Breaks Ground on 27,000 SF Retail Project in Manvel, Texas
articles.narrative: Malabar Hill Capital has commenced construction on The Market at Meridiana, a 27,000-square-foot neighborhood shopping center in Manvel, Texas. The project is strategically located at the entrance of the Meridiana master-planned community and is already 54 percent preleased, indicating strong tenant demand in the area.

data_points.address: Hwy 6 & Meridiana Pkwy, Manvel, TX
data_points.notable_features: Located at the entrance of the Meridiana master-planned community.

articles.title: Plenty of momentum: Suburban Chicago multifamily market gains strength in first quarter of 2026
articles.narrative: The suburban Chicago multifamily market experienced significant growth in the first quarter of 2026, with total sales volume surging by 56.9% year-over-year to $360.1 million. This increase was driven by investors pursuing larger apartment assets, as the number of transactions remained flat at 54 deals compared to the previous year. The average transaction size rose to $6.6 million, and the total number of apartment units traded increased by 50.4% to 2,430 units, indicating a trend towards larger, more institutional-quality properties.
articles.market_intelligence: The Chicago suburban multifamily market saw a significant increase in sales volume and average transaction size in Q1 2026, indicating a shift towards larger, more institutional-quality properties. Cook County accounted for half of all suburban multifamily transactions, with DuPage and Kane counties showing larger-than-average deal sizes.

articles.title: Habitat, M/I Homes of Chicago to develop 227-residence community on former Solo Cup site in Highland Park
articles.narrative: A joint venture between Habitat and M/I Homes of Chicago has acquired the former Solo Cup Company site in Highland Park, Illinois, with plans to develop The Bowery of Highland Park, a 227-residence for-sale townhome community. The 28-acre vacant site will be transformed into a residential neighborhood featuring townhomes, preserved open space, landscaped parkways, and community amenities. The developers are moving forward with permitting and pre-construction activities, with groundbreaking anticipated for summer 2026, addressing the high demand for new-construction housing in established suburban locations.

data_points.address: 1700 Old Deerfield Road, Highland Park, IL
data_points.notable_features: Community amenities include a resident clubhouse with an outdoor pool, a dog park, a publicly accessible tot lot, and pedestrian paths. The site is bordered by preserved wetlands and mature trees.
data_points.project_notes: 34 homes will be offered to qualifying individuals and families in accordance with Highland Park’s Inclusionary Housing Plan. The site was previously the Solo Cup Company site.

articles.title: Cushman & Wakefield, JLL negotiate sale of two-property self-storage portfolio in Chicago
articles.narrative: Etude Storage Capital has acquired a two-property, Class A self-storage portfolio in Chicago from Self Storage 1. The portfolio, totaling 150,216 square feet and 2,043 units, is strategically located in the affluent and supply-constrained Lincoln Park/Bucktown and Wrigleyville/Lakeview neighborhoods. This transaction underscores the strong investor demand for high-quality, well-located self-storage assets in major gateway cities, driven by durable demand from high incomes, renter density, and limited new supply in Chicago's core urban infill locations.

data_points.notable_features: The portfolio consists of two Class A self-storage facilities located in supply-constrained, affluent neighborhoods, benefiting from proximity to a major mixed-use redevelopment and a prominent sports stadium.

articles.market_intelligence: The average three-mile population across the portfolio exceeds 480,000 residents, with average household incomes above $164,000, indicating strong underlying submarket fundamentals.

articles.title: JLL Capital Markets closes $141 million refinancing for Wolf Point West in Chicago
articles.narrative: JLL Capital Markets has arranged a $141.817 million refinancing loan for Wolf Point West, a 48-story luxury multifamily tower in Chicago's River North neighborhood. The three-year, floating-rate loan was secured through New York Life Insurance Company on behalf of the borrower, a partnership between Hines, AFL-CIO Building Investment Trust (advised by DWS Group), Magellan Development Group, and the Joseph P Kennedy Trust. The property, delivered in 2016, is a LEED Silver-certified building offering 509 units and is strategically located on the Wolf Point peninsula, providing excellent access to entertainment, retail, and major business districts.

data_points.address: 343 W Wolf Point Plaza, Chicago, IL
data_points.notable_features: LEED Silver-certified building with floor-to-ceiling windows, stainless steel appliances, and a wide range of amenities including an outdoor pool, fitness center, and dog lounge. Located on the Wolf Point peninsula at the confluence of the Chicago River's three branches, offering immediate pedestrian access to entertainment and retail corridors and connectivity to premier business districts.

articles.market_intelligence: Chicago leads the nation in multifamily performance, with 4.2% annual rent growth compared to the national average of 0.4%. The city has been ranked as the top metropolitan area for corporate relocation and expansion by Site Selection Magazine for nine consecutive years.

articles.title: King County ditches lease for ownership in mental health services strategy
articles.narrative: King County has acquired the Connections Kirkland crisis center in Kirkland, WA, for $32.6 million, transitioning from a long-term lease to direct ownership of the 67,000-square-foot facility. This acquisition is part of a broader strategy to expand the county's behavioral health network, funded by a voter-approved property tax levy. The move is expected to save the county nearly $2.3 million in future lease payments and provides flexibility for potential expansion of services.

data_points.address: 11410 Northeast 122nd Way, Kirkland, WA
data_points.project_notes: The county had originally planned to lease the property through 2039. The property was previously acquired by the seller in 2019 for $35.7 million. The acquisition was funded by a nearly $1.3 billion property tax levy approved by voters in 2023.

articles.title: Housing Notes: When cash is king, Albany wants a cut
articles.narrative: This article analyzes a proposed 1% tax in New York on all-cash real estate purchases over $1 million, arguing that it is likely a legislative effort to recover lost mortgage recording tax revenue due to a surge in cash transactions. The author notes that approximately 75% of Manhattan purchases over $1 million are all-cash, representing an estimated $17.4 billion in 2025, and that this tax would affect a significant portion of high-end buyers. While the real estate industry views it as another tax burden that could slow sales, the author suggests it's a corrective measure to offset revenue declines from the mortgage recording tax, especially given the rise in cash transactions between 2022 and 2025. The article also highlights that co-op purchases, typically exempt from mortgage recording tax, would be subject to this new cash tax.

articles.title: Brooklyn luxury contracts come back to earth with $80M week
articles.narrative: Brooklyn's luxury residential market experienced a significant slowdown last week, with contract volume dropping to $80 million across 25 deals, following two consecutive weeks exceeding $150 million. The median asking price for these contracts was $2.5 million, with homes spending an average of 62 days on the market. The priciest contract was for a Carroll Gardens townhouse at 181 President Street, asking $8.3 million, while a unit at 110 Boerum Place in Cobble Hill secured the second-highest price at $5.6 million.

data_points.address: 181 President Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: The townhouse features a 29-foot-wide living room with 12-foot ceilings, floor-to-ceiling glass doors leading to a landscaped back terrace with a kitchenette, a primary suite with Lower Manhattan views, a private elevator, roof deck, multi-car garage, media room, and wine cellar.

articles.title: How pied-à-terre, cash-buyer surcharges will change behavior
articles.narrative: This article analyzes the potential behavioral changes and market impacts resulting from New York's proposed pied-à-terre and cash-buyer taxes. The author argues that these new taxes, intended to generate revenue and fulfill political pledges, are likely to discourage luxury home purchases, potentially shifting demand towards high-end rentals and impacting development decisions. The piece highlights the counterintuitive nature of taxing a behavior (owning a pied-à-terre) that the government simultaneously seeks to encourage for revenue, and suggests that wealthy buyers will likely seek methods of tax avoidance. The author also criticizes the rushed and opaque legislative process behind these tax proposals.

articles.title: PMG, partners launch Palm Tree-branded Miami condo project
articles.narrative: PMG, Lion Development Group, Eden Residential, and Sterling Equities have partnered to launch Palm Tree Residences Miami, a 37-story, 483-unit condominium project in Miami. This development marks the first branded residence for Palm Tree Crew, the music festival company co-founded by DJ Kygo. The project is designed to be short-term rental friendly, catering to investor demand in the Miami market.

data_points.address: 31 Northwest 10th Street, Miami, FL
data_points.notable_features: First branded residence for Palm Tree Crew; units are fully furnished and short-term rental friendly; amenities include a pool, fitness center, padel court, spa facilities, golf simulator, and juice bar.
data_points.project_notes: Prices for units range from $500,000 to $1.8 million. Palm Tree Crew will operate a dining and nightlife venue on the ground floor.

articles.market_intelligence: Miami has seen a recent surge in short-term rental-friendly condo projects, including Forth Development and MICL's Botanic Residences (51 units) and Wave Group Development, Vitrium Capital, and Zambala Luxury Apartments' Duos Wynwood (49 units).

articles.title: Which Miami-Dade office buildings are setting records? We mapped them
articles.narrative: Miami-Dade County's office market is experiencing record-setting rents, primarily driven by demand for smaller, high-end boutique office buildings rather than traditional corporate towers. These projects, often five or six stories, feature bespoke designs and luxury amenities, catering to ultra-high-net-worth individuals seeking discreet office space near their waterfront residences. While a major corporate tower, 830 Brickell, is finalizing a lease at $250 per square foot, most other record-breaking deals are occurring in these smaller, amenity-rich properties, with many letters of intent exceeding $200 per square foot and signed deals regularly surpassing $150 per square foot.

articles.title: Vinayaka Hospitality snags “Hotel of Presidents” for $43M
articles.narrative: Vinayaka Hospitality has acquired the historic 355-key Blackstone hotel in Chicago's Loop for $43 million from Fundamental Advisors and Sage Hospitality Group. The sale represents a significant discount from its previous purchase price in 2016, despite substantial renovations made by the sellers. The transaction occurs amidst a broader effort by Chicago's tourism board to boost the hospitality sector through a new surcharge.

data_points.address: 636 South Michigan Avenue, Chicago, IL
data_points.notable_features: Historic 'Hotel of Presidents' and official Chicago landmark, part of Marriott's Autograph Collection.
data_points.project_notes: Sellers acquired the property for $59.5 million in 2016 and invested approximately $12 million in renovations.

articles.market_intelligence: Chicago's tourism board is implementing a 1.5 percent surcharge on hotel stays to double the budget of tourism agency Choose Chicago.

articles.title: Iconic Mag Mile venue converting 400K sf into a Marriott
articles.narrative: Hearn is in advanced discussions with Marriott International to convert approximately 400,000 square feet of office space within the former John Hancock Center into a 350-key Edition-branded luxury boutique hotel. This proposed conversion, spanning floors 23 through 34, represents a strategic pivot for the iconic Magnificent Mile property, which has faced significant office vacancy challenges since the pandemic. The move aligns with broader efforts to revitalize Chicago's tourism industry, as other downtown venues are also being repurposed for hotel use.

data_points.address: 875 North Michigan Avenue, Chicago, IL
data_points.notable_features: Luxury boutique hotel conversion within an iconic 100-story mixed-use tower on the Magnificent Mile.
data_points.project_notes: Office space previously marketed as 'Mag Mile Medical Pavilion' for medical tenants. Hearn controls naming rights to the hotel, which lost the 'John Hancock Center' moniker in 2018.

articles.market_intelligence: The Chicago City Council approved a 1.5 percent surcharge on downtown hotel room rates in May, adding to an existing 17.5 percent tax, to boost the Choose Chicago tourism budget. The lower 10 floors of the BMO Harris Bank building are also being converted into hotels.

articles.title: Evanston moves forward with housing plan focused on raising housing density, cutting red tape
articles.narrative: The Evanston City Council has approved its Housing4All plan, a 134-page blueprint aimed at increasing housing affordability and density. The plan seeks to preserve 1,000 existing affordable units, construct 4,000 new ones, and protect residents from displacement. A key component involves pursuing greater housing density across all neighborhoods, with the council rejecting amendments that would have limited redevelopment in lower-density areas. This initiative comes as Evanston grapples with rising housing costs, which have contributed to economic and racial segregation, with an estimated 35% of households considered cost-burdened in 2025.

data_points.project_notes: The Housing4All plan aims to preserve 1,000 existing affordable housing units and construct 4,000 new ones.

articles.title: Landlord Raphael Lowenstein hit With $35M Bronzeville foreclosure
articles.narrative: A $35 million foreclosure lawsuit has been filed against Raphael Lowenstein for Drexel Terraces, a 116-unit multifamily property in Chicago's Bronzeville neighborhood. Loan servicers Trimont and LNR Partners initiated the suit after the Lowenstein entity that owns the property allegedly stopped making loan payments in December. The original $31 million loan, issued by Morgan Stanley in February 2024, was subsequently securitized. The property's appraised value has significantly declined from $50.2 million in early 2024 to $31 million in April, despite a 99% occupancy rate, due to poor net operating income and a low debt service coverage ratio.

data_points.address: 4830-4848 ½ South Drexel Boulevard, Chicago, IL
data_points.project_notes: The original loan for $31 million was issued by Morgan Stanley in February 2024 before being pooled into a CMBS deal. The property's appraised value declined from $50.2 million in early 2024 to $31 million in April 2026. The City of Chicago and an unpaid plumbing contractor are also named as defendants to clear junior liens.

articles.title: Development likely for almost 500 acres of Weatherford “horse country”
articles.narrative: Aledo-based homebuilder Kenmark Homes, operating as NTX Crowder Ranch Development LLC, has acquired a 487-acre estate in Weatherford, TX, from the heirs of John and Helen Crowder. The undeveloped property, previously listed for $11 million, sold above its asking price and is expected to be developed for residential use. The transaction highlights the increasing demand for land in Parker County, a region known for its equestrian community.

data_points.notable_features: Largely unimproved ranch with stock tanks, a hay barn, hilly terrain, and mature hardwood coverage.
data_points.project_notes: The land was listed for $11 million and sold above its asking price.

companies_people.label: SELLER
companies_people.firm_name: Heirs of John and Helen Crowder

articles.title: Franklin family looks to ease parking pressure on Fort Worth’s Stockyards with $14M garage
articles.narrative: The Franklin family, owners of M.L. Leddy's, received zoning approval for a new four-story parking garage in Fort Worth's historic Stockyards. The $14 million project aims to alleviate significant parking shortages in the popular tourist area, which a 2023 study found to be operating at over 100% parking capacity on key streets. The proposed garage will include 246 spaces along with ground-floor retail and office space, with construction potentially starting after the FIFA World Cup in July.

data_points.address: 115 NW 25th Street and 2458 Ellis Avenue, Fort Worth, TX
data_points.notable_features: Four-story parking garage with 246 spaces and ground-floor retail and office space.
data_points.project_notes: The project is a fraction of a previously paused, larger Stockyards expansion plan that included 1,300 underground parking spaces.

articles.title: San Jose motel eyed for housing conversion as Mahan touts lodging-to-living push
articles.narrative: A motel in north San Jose is slated for conversion into a residential complex, aligning with the city's strategy to meet state housing goals. The project involves transforming the existing 58-room Caravelle Inn & Suites into 58 apartments, followed by the construction of a new five-story, 45-unit apartment building on the same parcel. This initiative is part of San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan's broader effort to convert motels into housing across the city.

data_points.address: 1310 North First Street, San Jose, CA
data_points.notable_features: The project will convert an existing 58-room hotel into 58 apartments and add a new 45-unit apartment building.
data_points.project_notes: The project will unfold in two phases, with the first phase converting the existing hotel and the second phase adding a new building.

articles.title: Align beefs up Safeway redevelopment plans in SF’s Outer Richmond
articles.narrative: Align Real Estate has submitted revised plans for the redevelopment of a Safeway grocery store in San Francisco's Outer Richmond neighborhood. The updated proposal calls for a larger mixed-use project featuring 562 residential units, an increase from the previously planned 526 units, along with expanded retail space. The project incorporates affordable housing units and utilizes state density bonus laws to facilitate the development.

data_points.address: 850 La Playa Street, San Francisco, CA
data_points.notable_features: The project includes 113 deed-restricted affordable housing units for residents earning 50-80% of the area's median income.
data_points.project_notes: The application invokes Senate Bill 330 and the state's density bonus law to increase residential capacity and streamline the approval process.

articles.title: Omnichannel Retailer Cozey Will Open First U.S. Store in Flatiron District
articles.narrative: Omnichannel furniture retailer Cozey is expanding its presence in the U.S. market by opening its first permanent store in Manhattan's Flatiron District. The Canadian direct-to-consumer brand signed a 10-year lease for 10,146 square feet at 160 Fifth Ave., a mixed-use building owned by RFR. This move signifies a growing trend of digitally native brands establishing physical retail footprints in key urban markets like New York City.

data_points.address: 160 Fifth Ave., New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Space previously occupied by Club Monaco and Ralph's Coffee.
data_points.project_notes: This marks Cozey's first permanent U.S. store, following a pop-up in 2024.

articles.title: Berkadia Lines Up Construction Loan for Seachange Partners
articles.narrative: Berkadia has secured $15.6 million in non-recourse construction financing for Seachange Partners, facilitating the development of two affordable housing projects in Los Angeles. The five-story developments, located in the Sawtelle and Palms neighborhoods, will collectively provide 79 deed-restricted affordable units. The lender was Genesis Capital LLC, a private residential construction lender based in Los Angeles. The projects are currently under construction and are expected to be completed in 2027.

data_points.address: 1723 Corinth Ave, Los Angeles, CA / 3751 Delmas Terr, Los Angeles, CA
data_points.notable_features: Deed-restricted affordable housing projects.
data_points.project_notes: These represent the developer's inaugural projects.

articles.title: Ally Bridge Relocates U.S. Headquarters to Rudin’s 560 Lexington
articles.narrative: Ally Bridge Group, a global investment management firm specializing in healthcare and life science, has signed a 10-year, 10-month lease to relocate its U.S. headquarters to 560 Lexington in New York City's Plaza District. The firm will occupy 6,440 square feet on the 18th floor of the 22-story office tower, with the move expected in early 2027. This long-term commitment underscores the Plaza District's continued appeal to the global investment community.

data_points.address: 560 Lexington, New York City, NY
data_points.project_notes: Ally Bridge Group is relocating from 430 Park Ave.

articles.title: Cleveland Unveils 350-Acre Industrial Redevelopment Project
articles.narrative: The City of Cleveland has unveiled "The Midline," a significant 350-acre industrial redevelopment project on the city's near east side. This initiative aims to transform long-vacant industrial land into a connected employment district, featuring new trails, parks, and enhanced transit access. The project, spearheaded by The Site Readiness for Good Jobs Fund, will consolidate fragmented parcels to create a unified district designed to attract large-scale employers in manufacturing, R&D, office, and service sectors. The master plan envisions at least 1.5 million square feet of new industrial and commercial space, projected to create over 2,500 direct jobs and generate up to $100 million in annual tax revenue for the city upon full build-out.

data_points.notable_features: Includes new trails, parks, and transit access; designed to attract large-scale employers across manufacturing, research and development, office and supporting service sectors.
data_points.project_notes: The project is a district-wide strategy rather than a single-user project, driven by The Site Readiness for Good Jobs Fund (SRF).

companies_people.label: DEVELOPER/SPONSOR
companies_people.firm_name: The Site Readiness for Good Jobs Fund (SRF)

articles.title: Gould Property Co., Konterra Realty Break Ground on 400-Acre MD Project
articles.narrative: Gould Property Co. and Konterra Realty have broken ground on the initial phase of the Konterra Town Center East project in Prince George's County, Maryland. This first phase includes the construction of 220 townhomes, with the first units expected to deliver in fall 2026, and two apartment buildings totaling 500 units, slated for completion in 2028. The development is part of a larger 1,400-acre master-planned community, transforming a former mining site into a significant mixed-use destination.

data_points.notable_features: Part of a larger 1,400-acre master-planned community on both sides of I-95, on a site formerly used for sand and gravel mining operations.
data_points.project_notes: The Gould family has owned the land since the early 1980s. The first phase includes 220 townhomes and 500 apartment units.

articles.title: 10-Year Clears 4.5% 'Ceiling,' Forcing Real Estate To Reckon With Rate Reality
articles.narrative: The U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield recently surpassed 4.5%, reaching its highest point since January 2025, prompting varied reactions within the commercial real estate sector. While some experts view this as a psychological rather than a material shift, others consider it a critical juncture, particularly for the substantial volume of pre-2022 debt maturing this year. The elevated rates are making loan sizing challenging and impacting acquisition returns, with concerns that a sustained increase above 5% could significantly impede market improvement. Despite these headwinds, capital is flowing back into real estate, with strong investment sales and office leasing activity reported in the first quarter, suggesting a market that is adapting to the new rate environment.

people.name: Jim Costello
people.title: co-heads the real assets research team

articles.market_intelligence: The CBRE Lending Momentum Index reached a five-year high in the first quarter of 2026. U.S. office leasing achieved a decade high in the first quarter. Investment sales nationwide totaled $112.6B in Q1 2026, an 18% year-over-year increase, with projections for total 2026 activity to reach $588B.

articles.title: ZOM Ordered To Pay $323M For Circumventing Partner In Arizona Multifamily JV
articles.narrative: A Maricopa County jury has ordered ZOM Living to pay $323 million in damages to Gray Development Group for breach of contract and implied good faith and fair dealing. The dispute arose from a 2019 joint venture agreement where Gray Development shared proprietary information for five planned multifamily projects in Arizona. Gray Development alleged that ZOM Living subsequently circumvented the agreement, using Gray's confidential data to develop the projects independently after Gray ceased operations in 2020. The projects, consisting of four apartment communities in Phoenix and Scottsdale, broke ground last year and are slated for 2027 delivery.

data_points.notable_features: Upscale multifamily project located within the Compass master-planned community, Desert Ridge, near Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.'s upcoming manufacturing center.
data_points.project_notes: The 33 acres of land for the projects were acquired by a ZOM affiliate through U.S. Bankruptcy Court in 2021. The projects were originally part of Gray Development's planned $1.4 billion development pipeline of 13 projects.

articles.title: Kygo's Music Festival Company Latest Brand To Adorn Miami Condos
articles.narrative: Superstar DJ Kygo and his manager Myles Shear, through their company Palm Tree Crew, are partnering with developers Property Markets Group, Lion Development, Eden Residential, and Sterling Equities to develop Palm Tree Residences Miami, a 37-story condominium tower in Downtown Miami. The Kobi Karp-designed project will feature 483 short-term-rental-friendly units and is expected to open by 2030. This development highlights a growing trend in Miami's luxury real estate market where recognizable brands are used to differentiate projects and command a premium.

data_points.address: 31 NW 10th St, Miami, FL
data_points.notable_features: The project will host monthly electronic and house music events, and owners will receive priority access to Palm Tree Crew events and venues worldwide. Amenities include an infinity rooftop pool, padel court, wellness center, yoga studio, juice bar, and golf simulator.
data_points.project_notes: Units are designed to be short-term-rental-friendly and will range in price from $500,000 to $1.8 million.

articles.market_intelligence: Miami is the second-largest global market for branded residential projects, with developers reporting that a recognizable brand can command a 15% to 20% premium over non-branded projects.

articles.title: ULI Shuffles Offices For Global HQ, Washington Chapter
articles.narrative: The Urban Land Institute (ULI) has relocated its global headquarters to 2101 L St. NW in Washington, D.C., securing 26,000 square feet. This move, from its previous 33,000-square-foot space at 2001 L St. NW, aims to foster team collaboration and strengthen ULI's mission. Concurrently, ULI Washington, the local chapter, has moved into an 800-square-foot sublease within Hickok Cole's headquarters in the NoMa submarket, a strategic shift to decentralize its regional events.

data_points.address: 2101 L St. NW, Washington, D.C.
data_points.notable_features: Building renovated in 2007, designed for natural light, accessibility, and a balance of shared and focused workspaces.
data_points.project_notes: ULI Washington's 800 SF sublease is at Hickok Cole's headquarters, a former 1930s-era printing press warehouse.

articles.title: Boston Office Market's Big Problem? Not Enough Demolition, Execs Say
articles.narrative: Boston's office market is performing better than its high vacancy rate suggests, according to market leaders, who argue that the overall vacancy figure is inflated by a large stock of obsolete buildings. Tenant demand for high-quality, Class A office space in downtown Boston and close-in suburbs remains robust, with some new office buildings achieving unprecedented rents. The market is also seeing a positive trend of adaptive reuse, with numerous office-to-residential conversions approved or underway, which is helping to reduce the supply of less desirable office space. This flight to quality and the redevelopment of older assets are expected to lead to a healthier market as companies continue to bring employees back to the office.
